numerical_claims.notes: Within 0.5% tolerance. The 60.8 mK in Table 2 is more precise and also correct.
Within tolerance. The 3.0 is a round number; exact calc gives 3.013.
Match. Note: this is PROJECTED, not measured.
Close to tolerance boundary but acceptable for an approximate value in text.
Rounded to 1 sig fig. Actual is 0.00187. The paper value 0.002 is ~7% high but acceptable as a rough estimate. Should use 0.0019 for precision.

numerical_claims.location: Section III, line 188: SNR ~ 11 with 100 photons
numerical_claims.notes: Consistent within rounding to 2 sig figs.

numerical_claims.computed_value: 0.30 (all three platforms share the same c_s, xi, kappa => same D)
numerical_claims.notes: The abstract says 0.3-0.5 but all three platforms have D = 0.30. There is no platform with D = 0.5 in the codebase. The range is misleading.

numerical_claims.location: Abstract, lines 27-28: ~20% dispersive corrections to T_H
numerical_claims.notes: CRITICAL: At D=0.30, the dispersive correction is ~9%, not ~20%. The ~20% value corresponds to D~0.46 (old parameters before c_s correction). The abstract contradicts the paper body (Section III, line 149) which correctly says ~10%. The formulas.py docstring for dispersive_hawking_correction also has the stale D=0.46 value.

CRITICAL: The abstract claim is numerically false. G(0.3k) = 0.18, NOT > 0.5. The crossover G=0.5 occurs at omega/kappa ~ 0.175. Must correct to 'G > 0.5 for omega < 0.17*kappa' or 'G > 0.1 for omega < 0.3*kappa'.

theorem_refs.name: spatial_attenuation_ge_one (paper abstract, line 37)
theorem_refs.notes: Name mismatch: paper says 'spatial_attenuation_ge_one', actual Lean name is 'attenuation_ge_one'. Also, the paper (Section VI, line 267) claims Aristotle 'da7cb04d' proved this, but da7cb04d is the HubbardStratonovichRHMC run. The PolaritonTier1 theorems are NOT in ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS -- they were proved manually.

theorem_refs.name: tier1_regime_monotone (paper abstract, line 38)
theorem_refs.notes: Name mismatch: paper says 'tier1_regime_monotone', actual Lean name is 'attenuation_mono_Gamma'.

theorem_refs.name: bec_recovery_limit (paper abstract, line 38)
theorem_refs.notes: Name mismatch: paper says 'bec_recovery_limit', actual Lean name is 'attenuation_eq_one_at_zero_decay'.

theorem_refs.name: stimulated_hawking_gain (paper abstract, line 39)
theorem_refs.notes: The formulas.py docstring references 'stimulated_hawking_gain_pos (future)' as the Lean theorem, but this theorem does not exist in any .lean file. The paper lists this as a verified formula but the Lean formalization is missing.

theorem_refs.name: polariton_hawking_temperature (paper abstract, line 40)
theorem_refs.notes: formulas.py correctly delegates to hawking_temperature(), which references hawking_temp_from_surface_gravity in AcousticMetric.lean. This theorem exists and is proved.

theorem_refs.name: hawking_temp_from_surface_gravity (paper Section VI)
theorem_refs.notes: Exists in AcousticMetric.lean line 282, fully proved (rfl).

theorem_refs.name: stimulated_hawking_snr (paper Section VI, line 259)
theorem_refs.notes: The formulas.py docstring says 'stimulated_hawking_snr_scaling (future)' -- this Lean theorem does not exist. However, the paper does not explicitly claim this is verified, and the formula (sqrt(N)*G) is trivially correct. WARN because the paper's Section VI says 'All numerical predictions derive from formulas.py' without distinguishing verified from unverified formulas.

theorem_refs.name: 1214 Lean 4 theorems (abstract line 34, conclusion line 289)
theorem_refs.notes: CRITICAL: Theorem count is wrong. Actual count from grep: 1187 theorems across 84 .lean files (excluding .backup). The constants.py header says 1179. The paper claims 1214 which matches neither. Must be corrected to the actual count.

theorem_refs.name: 0 axioms (abstract line 35)
theorem_refs.notes: Confirmed: grep for '^axiom ' in lean/SKEFTHawking/*.lean returns 0 matches. AXIOM_METADATA shows all former axioms have been removed.

theorem_refs.name: PolaritonTier1.lean: 6 theorems, zero sorry (abstract line 36)
theorem_refs.notes: Confirmed: PolaritonTier1.lean has exactly 6 theorems and 0 sorry.

qualitative_claims.support: T_H_polariton ~ 61 mK vs T_H_BEC ~ 0.35 nK => ratio ~ 1.74e8
qualitative_claims.notes: The ratio 61 mK / 0.35 nK = 1.74e8, which is ~10^8, not 10^9. The abstract range '0.1-1 K' would give 10^8-10^9. At 61 mK the advantage is ~10^8. The claim '10^9' overstates by an order of magnitude at the actual predicted T_H. Should say '10^8'.

qualitative_claims.claim: Stimulated detection 10^3-10^6 times better than spontaneous (abstract line 29, Section III line 189)
qualitative_claims.support: SNR(stimulated, 100 photons) ~ 11 vs SNR(spontaneous) requires ~10^9 shots. The comparison is not apples-to-apples: the 10^3-10^6 factor is not a direct computation output.
qualitative_claims.notes: The claim is qualitatively supported by Grisins (2016) but the exact 10^3-10^6 range is not directly computed from the pipeline. It depends on the spontaneous baseline which comes from external references.

qualitative_claims.claim: Optimal probe window is omega < 0.3*kappa (Section III, line 167)
qualitative_claims.support: G(0.3k) = 0.18, which is >10% of a unit-intensity probe. The 'optimal' designation is reasonable.
qualitative_claims.notes: The text correctly defines 'optimal' as 'stimulated signal exceeds 10% of probe intensity', and G(0.3k)=0.18 > 0.10. But this contradicts the abstract claim of G>0.5 at omega<0.3k.

qualitative_claims.claim: Only ~100 probe photons per mode needed for 5-sigma detection (abstract line 33)
qualitative_claims.notes: Correct at omega = 0.1*kappa. At higher frequencies, more photons needed.

qualitative_claims.claim: Ultralong (300 ps) is optimal; standard (3 ps) is 'Marginal' (Table 3)
qualitative_claims.support: kappa*tau: ultralong=15, standard=0.15. Condition kappa*tau>1 satisfied only by long and ultralong.

qualitative_claims.claim: Noise T ~ 1.3 K 'does not affect the stimulated measurement' (line 230)
qualitative_claims.support: T_noise/T_H ~ 1.3K/0.061K ~ 21. The claim that stimulated measurement bypasses this noise is qualitatively supported by Grisins (2016).
qualitative_claims.notes: Physical argument is sound -- stimulated measurement is at mean-field level.

qualitative_claims.support: Same issue as above: actual ratio is ~10^8.
qualitative_claims.notes: See qualitative_claims[0]. Should be ~10^8 at 61 mK.

axiom_risk.reason: All axioms have been removed from the Lean codebase. AXIOM_METADATA shows 2 entries, both with eliminability='removed'. The paper correctly claims 0 axioms.

hypothesis_risk.nature: Required for well-defined surface gravity. Standard BEC physics.
affected_claims: All Hawking temperature and gain calculations

hypothesis_risk.nature: Cavity decay rate is non-negative. Physical.
Positive group velocity. Standard BEC physics.
Physics assumption that cavity decay is perturbative relative to horizon dynamics. Checked computationally: Paris_long = 0.2, Paris_ultralong = 0.067.
affected_claims: All polariton predictions assume Tier 1 patch
hypothesis_risk.notes: Paris_long ratio = 0.2 is marginal for 'perturbative'. The code classifies it as 'excellent' only if < 0.03, 'perturbative' if < 0.1. At 0.2, it falls outside the perturbative regime by the code's own classification. However, the paper does not mention this threshold issue.

hypothesis_risk.hypothesis: Acoustic metric description holds in weak-loss, long-wavelength limit
hypothesis_risk.nature: Foundation of the analog gravity framework in driven-dissipative systems. Supported by Amelio2020.

additional_issues.issue: Theorem count '1214' does not match actual count
additional_issues.actual: 1187 theorems (grep '^theorem ' across 84 .lean files). Constants.py header says 1179. Neither matches 1214.
additional_issues.notes: Must reconcile. The count in constants.py and the actual grep count also disagree (1179 vs 1187), suggesting the constants.py header is also stale.

additional_issues.issue: Wrong Aristotle run ID for spatial_attenuation_ge_one
additional_issues.actual: Paper claims Aristotle 'da7cb04d' proved this theorem. But da7cb04d is the HubbardStratonovichRHMC run. PolaritonTier1 theorems are ALL manual -- none appear in ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS.
additional_issues.notes: The Aristotle attribution is completely wrong. The theorem was proved manually in Lean.

additional_issues.issue: All three named theorems use wrong names
additional_issues.actual: Paper: spatial_attenuation_ge_one -> Lean: attenuation_ge_one; Paper: tier1_regime_monotone -> Lean: attenuation_mono_Gamma; Paper: bec_recovery_limit -> Lean: attenuation_eq_one_at_zero_decay
additional_issues.notes: All three theorem names in the abstract are fabricated -- they do not exist in the Lean source.

additional_issues.actual: Docstring says 'D ~ 0.46 (our polariton system): ~20% correction'. After c_s correction, D = 0.30 with ~9% correction.
additional_issues.notes: The docstring was not updated when c_s was corrected from 1.0 to 0.5 um/ps.

additional_issues.actual: formulas.py says 'Lean: polariton_validity_nonneg'. Actual Lean name is 'validity_nonneg' in PolaritonTier1 namespace.
additional_issues.notes: The formulas.py Lean reference should use the fully qualified name or match the declaration.

additional_issues.issue: Amo2009 c_s value is for a different material system
additional_issues.actual: Amo (2009) measured a CdTe microcavity, not GaAs. The 0.81 um/ps value may not be directly comparable to the GaAs measurements.
additional_issues.notes: Table caption says 'GaAs microcavities' but Amo (2009) used CdTe. Should clarify.

additional_issues.issue: 12 of 18 bibliography entries missing from CITATION_REGISTRY
additional_issues.actual: Only Steinhauer2016, deNova2019, Falque2025, Grisins2016, Jacquet2022 are in the registry. Missing: Gerace2012, Nguyen2015, Estrecho2021, Stepanov2019, Claude2023, Burkhard2025, Finazzi2012, Amelio2020, Jacquet2023QNM, Nelsen2013, Alnatah2024, Amo2009.
additional_issues.notes: 12 citations need to be added to citations.py before submission.

additional_issues.issue: Tier changed from MEASURED to PROJECTED but paper Table 3 does not indicate 100 ps and 300 ps cavities are projections
additional_issues.actual: Falque actual cavity τ ~ 8 ps. The 100 ps and 300 ps are projected future cavities. Paper should clearly label these as projected.
additional_issues.notes: Table 2 labels kappa as 'Projected (SLM-controlled)' but does not label tau_cav as projected.
